name: LibreNMS MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server for LibreNMS — the popular open-source auto-discovering network monitoring system. Enables AI agents to query LibreNMS for device status, SNMP data, port statistics, alerts, and network topology — supporting AI-driven network operations, troubleshooting, and capacity planning workflows.
use_cases: Querying LibreNMS for network device status and port statistics from NetOps agents
Retrieving alerts and events from LibreNMS for network incident response agents
Analyzing network bandwidth and device health data from AI capacity planning agents
Investigating network topology and device discovery data for network audit agents
not_for: Teams not using LibreNMS (Zabbix, PRTG, SolarWinds users need different MCPs)
Cloud-only environments without physical network devices
Real-time streaming network metrics (LibreNMS API is request-response)
best_when: A network team using LibreNMS to monitor their network infrastructure wants AI agents to query device health, analyze port statistics, and assist with network troubleshooting.
avoid_when: You use Zabbix, Datadog, or other monitoring platforms. LibreNMS focuses on SNMP-based network device monitoring.

auth.notes: LibreNMS API token required. Generate in LibreNMS admin panel under API access.

pricing.notes: LibreNMS is free open source (GPL). MCP server is free open source.

performance.rate_limits: LibreNMS API: no explicit rate limits — bounded by server load

known_agent_gotchas: LibreNMS must be accessible via network — typically internal/on-premise deployment
Large LibreNMS deployments can have thousands of devices — use device-specific queries for performance
SNMP data freshness depends on polling interval (default 5 minutes) — not real-time
API token scope is global — cannot restrict to specific devices or groups
agent_readiness.security_notes: Network topology and device data is sensitive. API token security important. TLS to LibreNMS server recommended for external access.

security_score_components.security_notes: Network data sensitive. API token. TLS recommended. Typically on internal network.
